title: Model Division
essential_question: How can you use models to solve division problems?
strategies: Draw circles for each group, then deal counters one-by-one.
Use the total and number of groups to find how many in each group.

pretest.stem: Six customers bought 18 jump ropes. Each bought the same number. How many did each buy?
Hiro has 36 pictures. Each album page holds 4 pictures. How many pages does he need?
Katia puts 42 crayons into 6 equal sections. How many crayons in each section?
24 cards are shared equally among 3 helpers. How many cards each?
Jamie divides 20 stickers equally among 5 friends. How many each?

learn.title: What Is Division?
learn.content: Division means sharing equally or making equal groups. 18 ÷ 6 means: put 18 into 6 equal groups — how many in each group?

learn.title: Model with Counters
learn.content: To solve 18 ÷ 6: draw 6 circles. Place 1 counter in each, then repeat. When all 18 counters are used, count: 3 in each circle.

try.stem: Maria buys 15 apples and puts 5 in each bag. How many bags?
hints: Think about what model division means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how model division works.
